title: Tragacantha orbiculata
taxonomy: Tragacantha orbiculata belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a species of flowering plant commonly known as the globe milk thistle or globe thistle. This plant is native to Europe and Western Asia. Tragacantha orbiculata is a perennial herb with a distinctive globe-shaped inflorescence. It is often classified as a type of thistle, with the scientific name sometimes being revised to Carduus orbiculatus or Silybum orbiculatus.
uses: Tragacantha orbiculata, also known as Astragalus gummifer, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots are considered to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating different health issues. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive problems and respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also believed to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of its medicinal uses.
